title: Enum Classes
description: Create strict, limited lists of options.

slides.title: The Infinite String 🧵
slides.content:
## Typo Danger

Imagine tracking a user's subscription status: `var status: String = "ACTIVE"`.

Because it's a String, another developer might accidentally set it to `"Activ"` or `"Premium_Plus"`. Strings allow *infinite* possibilities, which leads to silent bugs when spelling is slightly wrong.

slides.title: The Enum Solution 🛑
slides.content:
An **Enum** (Enumeration) allows you to define a strictly limited, universally agreed-upon list of choices.

Now, your variables MUST be exactly one of those predefined choices. No typos allowed!

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of replacing Strings with an Enum class for categorical data?

options.text: It completely prevents typos by enforcing a strict, limited set of valid options at compile time.

quizData.explanation: If a developer tries to type `Plan.PREMUM`, the compiler will immediately catch the typo and refuse to build the app.

slides.title: Perfect pairs ✨
slides.content:
## Enums + When

Enums pair flawlessly with the `when` expression we learned earlier.

The compiler actually *knows* how many Enums exist. If you use `when` and forget to write an outcome for the `ENTERPRISE` tier, Kotlin will dynamically warn you that you missed one!
